name: MET-PREVENT metformin physical-performance frailty trial
summary: A double-blind randomized placebo-controlled trial testing 500 mg oral metformin three times daily for four months in older adults with probable sarcopenia and physical prefrailty or frailty.
population: Adults aged 65 years and older with slow 4-meter walk speed plus probable sarcopenia and physical prefrailty or frailty

trial_details.horizon_note: Results are already in the public evidence layer, so this is not an open no-result watch item.
trial_details.why_it_matters: Results matter for AMPK and Metabolic Modulators because they can show whether the intervention changes function and safety in Adults aged 65 years and older with slow 4-meter walk speed plus probable sarcopenia and physical prefrailty or frailty. That is still narrower than proof of broad aging reversal.
